findings.problem: The design's verification plan is out of sync with the task list: it stops at checkpoint-4 and folds observability/tooling into checkpoint-3, while the tasks require a separate checkpoint-4 for observability/tooling/board evidence and a final checkpoint-5 source-first gate. That leaves the staged verification flow ambiguous for implementers and reviewers.
findings.evidence: /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/bev-steering-perception-refactor/design.md:458-464 lists only artifact-completion plus source-first checkpoints 1-4, with checkpoint-3 covering "steering snapshot/media and host tooling" and checkpoint-4 covering only board smoke evidence. /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/bev-steering-perception-refactor/tasks.md:46-57 defines checkpoint-4 as the observability/tooling/board-evidence review and adds a separate final source-first checkpoint-5 for the completed implementation bundle.
findings.recommendation: Align the design's "Review Checkpoints" section with the tasks by restoring checkpoint-4 to observability/tooling/board evidence and adding checkpoint-5 as the final source-first verification gate, so the verification sequence has one unambiguous checkpoint map.

findings.problem: The modified observability spec does not require any accepted BEV overlay or dual-view artifact, even though the proposal, design, and tasks all rely on raw-image/BEV overlay support as a core verification surface for the BEV refactor.
findings.evidence: /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/bev-steering-perception-refactor/proposal.md:14 commits to updating the overlay probe. /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/bev-steering-perception-refactor/design.md:131-135 uses `scene_overlay_probe` raw/BEV dual view as a verification hook. /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/bev-steering-perception-refactor/tasks.md:29-30 and :49-50 require host-side overlay support and tooling that can render/check both raw-image and BEV overlays. But /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/bev-steering-perception-refactor/specs/steering-tuning-media-observability/spec.md:32-51 only requires the raw 320x240 frame envelope, steering snapshot metadata, and alignment metadata; it never makes a BEV overlay or equivalent reconstruction surface part of the accepted contract.
findings.recommendation: Add a requirement and scenario that define the accepted BEV overlay evidence surface, either by requiring a deterministic dual-view artifact or by requiring the metadata/projector outputs needed to reconstruct the BEV overlay from preserved evidence.

findings.problem: The change declares parameter/calibration surfaces as a primary impact area, but the task list never assigns explicit work to update parameter ownership, storage/loading, or accepted calibration artifacts. For a STRICT refactor, that leaves a material part of the implementation path unspecified.
findings.evidence: /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/bev-steering-perception-refactor/proposal.md:14, :28, and :32-33 call out parameter surfaces and `new/code/platform/param_store.cpp` as major impacted areas. /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/bev-steering-perception-refactor/specs/bev-steering-geometry/spec.md:19-24 requires camera/IPM calibration to remain a distinct contract. /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/bev-steering-perception-refactor/design.md:491-492 leaves the board calibration flow and near/far sampling table unresolved. Yet /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/bev-steering-perception-refactor/tasks.md:25-57 contains no task that names parameter schema updates, `param_store.cpp`, persisted calibration artifacts, or the accepted loading path for new BEV/FSM/control tuning knobs.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit tasks, and if needed companion spec/design text, for parameter ownership and persistence: where BEV projector calibration lives, how FSM/control thresholds are loaded, and what accepted artifact or table closes the tuning/calibration contract.
